texto: The more familiar acquaintance we have with God the more do we partake of him. He that passes by the fire may have some gleams of heat, but he that stands by it has his colour changed. It is not possible that a man should have any long conference with God and be no whit affected. If we are strangers to God it is no wonder that our faces become earthy.
explicacao: Orientação direta de William Bramwell sobre temas ligados a motivacao. A afirmação “The more familiar acquaintance we have with God the more do we partake of him. He that passes by …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, christian-life, christianity, útil em momentos de mudanca e inspiracao.

frase_original: The more familiar acquaintance we have with God the more do we partake of him. He that passes by the fire may have some gleams of heat, but he that stands by it has his colour changed. It is not possible that a man should have any long conference with God and be no whit affected. If we are strangers to God it is no wonder that our faces become earthy.
